Class RecurringEvent

java.lang.Object
planner.model.Event
planner.model.RecurringEvent
public class RecurringEvent extends Event
RecurringEvent is a specialized domain model class that represents an event with recurring scheduling rules.

Design Pattern - Inheritance: Uses the 'extends' keyword to inherit the structure and behavior of the Event superclass. This allows the base event class to be extended with new exclusive features (recurrenceType, seriesId), promoting code reuse and establishing a clear IS-A relationship.

Key Features:

  • Inherits all base event properties from Event
  • Adds recurrence type classification (Daily, Weekly, Monthly, None)
  • Maintains a series identifier to group recurring instances together
  • Calculates next occurrence based on recurrence type
  • Supports legacy data migration with optional series identifiers
  • Polymorphic serialization with recurrence metadata
Version:
2.0
Author:
Gustavo Henrique Nogueira de Andrade Filho, Pedro Rocha Dantas, Leonardo Oliveira Eid

    RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType
    Enumeration representing the possible recurrence patterns for a recurring event.

  • Constructor Details

    • RecurringEvent

      public RecurringEvent(String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutesBefore, RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType recurrenceType)
      Constructs a RecurringEvent with automatic series identifier generation.

      This constructor generates a new unique series identifier using UUID.randomUUID() for this recurring event. All occurrences of this recurring event will share the same series identifier to maintain the relationship between instances.

      Parameters:
      title - The title or name of the event (inherited from Event)
      dateTime - The LocalDateTime when the first occurrence of the event happens
      location - The physical or virtual location of the event
      description - A detailed description or notes about the event
      category - The category or classification of the event
      reminderMinutesBefore - The number of minutes before each occurrence to trigger a reminder
      recurrenceType - The RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType pattern for this recurring event

    • RecurringEvent

      public RecurringEvent(String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutesBefore, RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType recurrenceType, String seriesId)
      Constructs a RecurringEvent with an explicit series identifier.

      This constructor allows the specification of a custom series identifier, which is particularly useful for loading recurring events from persisted data or for migrating legacy recurring events that may not have an explicit series identifier.

      Legacy Support: A null seriesId is permitted to support legacy data loaded from older file formats. Such events will be automatically assigned a new series identifier during the data migration process.

      Parameters:
      title - The title or name of the event (inherited from Event)
      dateTime - The LocalDateTime when the first occurrence of the event happens
      location - The physical or virtual location of the event
      description - A detailed description or notes about the event
      category - The category or classification of the event
      reminderMinutesBefore - The number of minutes before each occurrence to trigger a reminder
      recurrenceType - The RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType pattern for this recurring event
      seriesId - A unique String identifier shared by all occurrences of this series, or null for legacy events without an explicit series identifier

  • Method Details

    • getRecurrenceType

      public RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType getRecurrenceType()
      Returns the recurrence type pattern for this recurring event.
      Returns:
      The RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType indicating how often this event repeats

    • setRecurrenceType

      public void setRecurrenceType(RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType recurrenceType)
      Sets the recurrence type pattern for this recurring event.
      Parameters:
      recurrenceType - The new RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType for this recurring event

    • getSeriesId

      public String getSeriesId()
      Returns the series identifier for this recurring event.

      The series identifier is a unique String that groups all occurrences of this recurring event together, allowing the system to identify which individual event instances belong to the same recurring series.

      Returns:
      The series identifier as a String, or null if the event is from legacy data without an explicit identifier

    • setSeriesId

      public void setSeriesId(String seriesId)
      Sets the series identifier for this recurring event.
      Parameters:
      seriesId - The unique String identifier to group this event with other occurrences of the same recurring series

    • calculateNextOccurrence

      public LocalDateTime calculateNextOccurrence()
      Calculates the date and time of the next occurrence of this recurring event.

      Based on the RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType associated with this object, calculates and returns the date/time of the next scheduled occurrence by adding the appropriate time interval to the current event's date/time:

      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.DAILY: Adds 1 day
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.WEEKLY: Adds 1 week
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.MONTHLY: Adds 1 month
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.NONE: Returns the same date/time (no recurrence)
      Returns:
      A LocalDateTime representing when the next occurrence should take place

    • toCSV

      public String toCSV()
      Converts the recurring event to a CSV (comma-separated values) format string for file persistence.

      Design Pattern - Polymorphism: This method overrides the Event.toCSV() method, demonstrating polymorphic behavior. The implementation calls super.toCSV() to reuse the parent class serialization logic, then appends a recurrence metadata suffix to identify this as a recurring event and preserve its series information.

      CSV Format Extension: The parent CSV format is extended with a suffix: ;[REC:RecurrenceType:SeriesId]

      Overrides:
      toCSV in class Event
      Returns:
      A formatted String in CSV format with recurrence metadata, ready for storage to disk
